div.p: Key Insight: No mutex needed! Atomic swap serializes all producers. Link step may be delayed but is safe.

g.text: next.store() | Release | Publishes node to consumer

ul.li: AcqRel on swap: both acquire previous state and release our node
Release on next.store: ensures data is visible before next pointer
Acquire on next.load: sees the data that was written before release

g.text: Send (contended) | ~50-200 ns | Cache line bounce on tail
Recv (data available) | ~20-50 ns | Load + pointer update
Recv (empty, blocks) | ~100-500 ns | Yield + wake overhead

ul.li: tail is hot: all producers write it
Causes cache line bouncing under contention
Mitigation: batch sends, or use per-producer buffers

text.tspan: // All producers race to swap tail
// All producers race to swap tail
// Link old tail to new node
// Link old tail to new node
// Step 3: Link (may be delayed, still safe)
// Step 3: Link (may be delayed, still safe)
// Step 3: Wake sender if capacity available
// Step 3: Wake sender if capacity available
